definition.name: GNS_construction
definition.definition: Let A be a unital *-algebra and φ ∈ S(A) a state. The Gelfand-Naimark-Segal (GNS) construction produces a triple (π_φ, H_φ, ξ_φ) as follows. Define a sesquilinear form on A by ⟨a, b⟩_φ = φ(b*a). Let N_φ = {a ∈ A : φ(a*a) = 0} (a left ideal, by Cauchy-Schwarz). The quotient A/N_φ is a pre-Hilbert space; its completion is the GNS Hilbert space H_φ. The left multiplication action of A on A/N_φ extends to a *-representation π_φ : A → B(H_φ). The cyclic vector is ξ_φ = [1] ∈ A/N_φ (the image of the identity), and it satisfies φ(a) = ⟨π_φ(a)ξ_φ, ξ_φ⟩ for all a ∈ A, and H_φ = closure(π_φ(A)ξ_φ) (cyclicity). The triple is unique up to unitary equivalence. Reference: Takesaki, Theory of Operator Algebras I, §I.9.
